Purpose: Assign a routing-time final decision role for one ambiguous TEP sample near the UTAR boundary.
Constrain the response to JSON only with decision in {normal, anomaly}.
Explain that the sample was escalated because it lies near the UTAR boundary and requires additional review.
State that missing an anomaly is costlier than flagging a borderline anomaly.
List strong and moderate anomaly cues and the conditions that trigger anomaly output.
Prevent over-reliance on perfect detector agreement and discourage default-normal behavior near the boundary.
Provide compact normal/anomaly routing examples in the same serialized feature format.
Provide categorical cues such as utar_side, detector votes, entropy level, discrepancy level, and graph concentration.
Provide RF, XGB, ModernTCN, UTAR-base, entropy, discrepancy, and temporal weighting signals.
Provide top sensors, structural score, z/trend/fluctuation, gap, and candidate topology evidence.
Appendix B prompt example is drawn from the representative routing path used for cost, latency, and prompt accounting.
